headline: HTSI editor’s letter: take a bow 2025
summary: Amadeus actor Will Sharpe stars in our final issue of the year
body_text:
I was obsessed with the film Amadeus when I was younger. Miloš Forman’s “fantasia” about a fictional rivalry between the prodigy Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart and the composer Antonio Salieri, adapted from the play by Peter Shaffer, came out in 1984 and won eight Academy Awards. I watched it on a scratchy VHS tape so many times that I could virtually recite the script by heart. I found it delightful that someone could be so implausibly naturally gifted – and at such a precocious age. Poor old Salieri, what a loser. Forever calcified in the effort of his labours, and never even getting close. The film was an early intergenerational drama. Mozart is deliciously Gen Z.

This week, Will Sharpe picks up the baton to play “Wolfie” in a five-part series also based on Shaffer’s play on Sky. It’s a canny piece of casting as the actor boasts prodigious talents of his own. A writer-director who does comedy and plays guitar and piano, he has worked constantly since graduating from Cambridge on such a di
